mission.title: litellm: [Bug]: `tool_choice` rejected when calling Azure GPT-5 models
mission.description: [Bug]: `tool_choice` rejected when calling Azure GPT-5 models. This issue affects 17+ users.

steps.title: Identify litellm troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your litellm deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n litellm -l app.kubernetes.io/name=litellm
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=litellm -n litellm --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review litellm configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant litellm configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n litellm -l app.kubernetes.io/name=litellm
kubectl get configmap -n litellm -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=litellm
```
### What happened?

When sending a chat completion request to **Azure GPT-5 reasoning models** through LiteLLM, requests that include `"tool_choice"` fail with:

`UnsupportedParamsError: azure does not support parameters: ['tool_choice']`

**Model

steps.title: Apply the fix for [Bug]: `tool_choice` rejected when calling Azure GPT-5…
steps.description:
## Support temperature for GPT-5 Chat model

GPT-5 chat is not an inference model, and since it requires support for parameters like temperature or tool_choice that are unavailable in inference models, I modified gpt_5_transformation to apply only when the model is a reasoning model.

Note: Since
```yaml
Error code: 400 - {'error_message': 'Chat completion failed', 'cause': "UnsupportedParamsError: azure does not support parameters: ['tool_choice'], for model=gpt-5-chat-2025-08-07. To drop these, set `drop_params=True` or for proxy: `litellm_settings: drop_params: true`. If you want to use these params dynamically send allowed_openai_params=['tool_choice'] in your request."}

....

Error code: 400 - {'error_message': 'Chat completion failed', 'cause': "UnsupportedParamsError: gpt-5 models don't support temperature=0.0. Only temperature=1 is supported. To drop unsupported params set `drop_param
```

steps.title: Confirm [Bug]: `tool_choice` rejected when calling Azure… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=litellm -n litellm --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n litellm --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
## Support temperature for GPT-5 Chat model

GPT-5 chat is not an inference model, and since it requires support for parameters like temperature or tool_choice that are unavailable in inference models, I modified gpt_5_transformation to apply only when the model is a reasoning model.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with litellm installed or the issue environment reproducible.
